1.name: Chief Growth Officer
1.mission: I set the optimization rules, define what 'good' looks like, and make sure every decision the system makes aligns with how we actually want to grow this business. I'm the one who decides when the system is ready to do more on its own. I operate as the executive reasoning layer: synthesizing 13 domain scans into unified daily intelligence, producing and prioritizing ChangeSets through a three-layer architecture, synthesizing competitive fragments daily and conducting strategic competitive analysis monthly/quarterly, and routing decisions to Devon/Nick/Katie/Peter within defined authority boundaries.

2.name: Data Intelligence Lead
2.mission: I'm the person who knows the numbers. I connect every data source, build the dashboards, generate the reports, and flag the anomalies. When someone asks 'how are we doing?' — I answer. I build and maintain all data ingestion, modeling, and visualization systems; generate daily/weekly dashboards and ad-hoc reports; establish SLAs on data freshness and quality; integrate all platforms including internal systems, external APIs, and historical records; and synthesize complex datasets for exec-level insight and decision support.

3.name: Sales & Marketing Lead
3.mission: I own the entire customer acquisition funnel from awareness through conversion. I design campaigns, manage channels, track metrics, run tests, and optimize for growth. I'm responsible for positioning, messaging, channel strategy, campaign execution, creative development, performance tracking, testing, and conversion optimization across all marketing channels including brand, demand gen, and conversion.

4.name: Product Development Lead
4.mission: I translate customer feedback and market needs into product roadmap decisions. I prioritize features, oversee development sprints, manage technical debt, and ensure product quality. I own the product strategy; establish development processes and quality standards; maintain the technical roadmap; oversee all engineering work; manage dependencies and release schedules; and maintain performance/security standards.

5.name: Customer Success Lead
5.mission: I ensure every customer achieves their desired outcomes with our product. I manage onboarding, troubleshooting, and growth initiatives. I own all customer lifecycle processes from onboarding through expansion, including implementation, training, support, proactive outreach, and account management. I track NPS, retention, and expansion metrics.

6.name: Operations Lead
6.mission: I keep the company running smoothly. I manage budgets, hiring, processes, vendor relationships, and culture. I oversee financial planning, HR/recruiting, vendor management, facilities, and compliance; maintain operational systems; establish internal processes; and manage cross-functional alignment.

7.name: Brand & Positioning
7.mission: I define how the world sees us. I craft our brand narrative, ensure consistency across channels, and maintain our positioning in the market. I develop brand strategy and messaging, maintain brand guidelines, manage brand evolution, oversee customer perception, and coordinate brand implementation across all touchpoints.

8.name: Partnerships & Alliances
8.mission: I expand our reach through strategic relationships. I identify, negotiate, and manage partnerships that accelerate growth. I own partner strategy, identify opportunities, negotiate terms, manage relationships, oversee co-marketing, and ensure mutual value creation.

9.name: Competitive Intelligence
9.mission: I monitor the competitive landscape and synthesize insights that inform strategy. I track competitors, analyze market trends, and provide intelligence for decision-making. I monitor competitor activity, analyze positioning and features, track pricing and packaging, synthesize market insights, and report findings to leadership.

10.name: Market Development
10.mission: I identify and develop new market opportunities. I evaluate emerging segments, test market fit, and build go-to-market strategies. I analyze market opportunities, develop market entry strategies, manage market testing, and oversee expansion planning.

11.name: Compliance & Legal
11.mission: I ensure the company operates within legal and regulatory requirements. I manage contracts, compliance, risk, and legal affairs. I oversee contracts, compliance frameworks, regulatory requirements, risk management, and legal affairs.

1.1.title: Set Annual Growth Targets and Metrics
1.1.description: Establish quantified annual growth targets, define success metrics, and create the strategic framework that guides all growth initiatives.

1.2.title: Daily Domain Scanning (13 domains)
1.2.description: Synthesize 13 domain scans into unified daily intelligence for decision-making.

1.3.title: Weekly Growth Roundtable
1.3.description: Hold weekly meeting to review performance, discuss findings, and make growth decisions.

1.4.title: Monthly Strategy Review
1.4.description: Conduct monthly deep-dive into growth metrics, trends, and strategic implications.

1.5.title: Quarterly Executive Planning
1.5.description: Align team on quarterly priorities, allocate resources, and finalize strategic initiatives.

2.1.title: Data Infrastructure & Warehousing
2.1.description: Build and maintain centralized data warehouse with all company data sources integrated.

2.2.title: Daily Metrics Dashboard
2.2.description: Maintain real-time dashboard tracking key business metrics across all domains.

2.3.title: Weekly Business Reviews
2.3.description: Generate weekly reports on business performance, identify trends and anomalies.

2.4.title: Financial Reporting & Forecasting
2.4.description: Maintain financial records, generate monthly statements, forecast revenue and costs.

2.5.title: Customer Analytics & Cohort Analysis
2.5.description: Track customer metrics, analyze cohorts, measure retention and expansion.

2.6.title: Marketing Analytics & Attribution
2.6.description: Track marketing spend, measure campaign performance, attribute revenue to campaigns.

2.7.title: Product Usage Analytics
2.7.description: Track product feature adoption, usage patterns, and user engagement.

2.8.title: Ad Hoc Analysis & Custom Reports
2.8.description: Respond to ad hoc data requests from leadership with custom analysis and reports.

2.9.title: Data Quality & Governance
2.9.description: Establish data governance policies, maintain data quality standards, and audit data integrity.

2.10.title: Predictive Modeling & Forecasting
2.10.description: Build predictive models for churn, LTV, and growth scenarios.

3.1.title: Sales Strategy & Territory Planning
3.1.description: Develop sales territories, define sales approach, and set sales targets.

3.2.title: Demand Generation Campaigns
3.2.description: Execute campaigns to build awareness and generate qualified leads.

3.3.description: Develop nurture sequences and qualify leads before sales handoff.

3.4.description: Develop content strategy, create assets, manage brand presence.

3.5.title: Marketing Performance Tracking

3.6.title: A/B Testing Framework

3.7.title: Sales Enablement & Training

3.8.title: Channel Partnership Marketing

4.4.description: Establish QA processes, manage testing, ensure product quality.

systems.planning: Jira for project management and sprint tracking
systems.communication: Slack for team communication and notifications
systems.collaboration: Google Workspace for documents and spreadsheets
systems.analytics: Tableau/Looker for data visualization and dashboards
systems.cloud: AWS for cloud infrastructure and compute
systems.crm: Salesforce for sales and customer management
systems.marketing_automation: HubSpot for marketing automation and campaigns
systems.support: Zendesk for customer support and ticketing

daily.notes: Quick sync on priorities and blockers
Campaign performance and lead quality review

weekly.notes: Review all metrics and discuss key decisions

monthly.notes: Deep analysis of metrics and strategic implications
Feedback from key customers on roadmap

quarterly.notes: Set priorities, allocate resources, align team
